title: podman stop for a container takes 10s
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> /kind bug


**Description**

Everytime I run `podman stop ...` the command takes ~10s to run.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. start the container

```console
# podman run -d alpine sleep 9999             
4ece26a2904da0184122f2540ed9810ddaaebdd4388b492b58c0cafc7c1b54c2                                         
```

2. see the running container

```console
# podman ps
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE    COMMAND      CREATED AT                      STATUS             PORTS   NAMES
4ece26a2904d   alpine   sleep 9999   2018-02-06 16:40:50 +0000 UTC   Up 5 seconds ago           brave_hugle
```

3. stopping the container

```console
# time podman stop brave_hugle                
4ece26a2904da0184122f2540ed9810ddaaebdd4388b492b58c0cafc7c1b54c2                                         

real    0m10.315s                                   
user    0m0.123s                                    
sys     0m0.049s                                    
```

**Describe the results you received:**

The deletion takes time.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Deletion can happen asynchronously (is that acheivable even though it is just a daemon)?


**Output of `podman version`:**

```console
# podman version                              
Version:       0.1
Go Version:    go1.9.2
Git Commit:    "bf00c976dd7509b7d84d1fa5254f1ac26fc494e5"
Built:         Tue Feb  6 14:58:14 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**


```console
# cat /etc/os-release 
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="27 (Cloud Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=27
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 27 (Cloud Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:27"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=27
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=27
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Cloud Edition"
VARIANT_ID=cloud
```

```console
# runc -v
runc version 1.0.0-rc4+dev
commit: 97b8bfe7fdea74d5e49b657013455a888328f04e
spec: 1.0.0
```


**CPU Profile**

file: 
[podman-stop.zip](https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/files/1700040/podman-stop.zip)

above was obtained as follows:

```console
# podman --cpu-profile podman-stop stop jolly_lalande 
af7134f26e5bded8b3f08f15f44d9c0053561cb6a0b75b6226c29efa5cc5582c

# go tool pprof podman-stop          
File: podman                                                                                                                                                                                                       
Build ID: 177139ae37cef8148c544bc1ba759ca5b69c382b  
Type: cpu                                                                                                
Time: Feb 6, 2018 at 5:07pm (UTC)                                                                        
Duration: 10.28s, Total samples = 20ms ( 0.19%)     
Entering interactive mode (type "help" for commands, "o" for options)
...
```

comments.body:
10s sounds like the default timeout for switching from the normal stop signal to SIGKILL. Makes me think that either the default signal is being switching to something that won't stop the container (or the container is catching our usual stop signal).

@surajssd Can you re-run `podman stop` with `--log-level=debug` and paste the output? It should announce what signal it's attempting to use in debug messages

Also attempt to modify the timeout.

podman stop -t 5 ...

SIGINT should be sent right away, and after 10 seconds by default SIGKILL will be sent.

@mheon, yes seems like the default time to stop the container is 10s, I should've investigated more before reporting issue:

```console
# podman --log-level=debug stop epic_lichterman
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay" 
DEBU[0000] overlay: override_kernelcheck=true           
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs,  projectQuotaSupported=false 
INFO[0000] CNI network mynet (type=bridge) is used from /etc/cni/net.d/10-mynet.conf 
DEBU[0000] Stopping ctr 6e0b11f7e3f716cbde0d2d3985933dcba23cee8b24e02874bd2c0e26b57bae67 with timeout 10 
WARN[0010] Timed out stopping container 6e0b11f7e3f716cbde0d2d3985933dcba23cee8b24e02874bd2c0e26b57bae67, resorting to SIGKILL 
DEBU[0010] unmounted container "6e0b11f7e3f716cbde0d2d3985933dcba23cee8b24e02874bd2c0e26b57bae67" 
6e0b11f7e3f716cbde0d2d3985933dcba23cee8b24e02874bd2c0e26b57bae67
```

also `-t` flag worked, @rhatdan

```console
# time podman stop -t 5 clever_visvesvaraya
1f0609a78b8d13099a364e068225916b63671de7651e04b79b09c7a27092eca9

real    0m5.280s
user    0m0.117s
sys     0m0.038s
```

Thanks for the quick replies
Well, if SIGINT is failing, it's potentially still a bug if we're dealing with sleep in the container. Sleep should not be catching and ignoring SIGINT.
I'm going to reopen this until we confirm.  10 seconds is a long time to wait if we don't have to.  @surajssd thanks for pointing this out.
I'm seeing this locally as well. SIGINT is not killing the container so it's always resorting to SIGKILL.
This also seems to be happening with `podman rm` on running containers - always hitting the 10 second timeout
Most likely we are not sending the SIGTERM at all.  Can verify by using podman kill instead of podman stop.

@rhatdan We're definitely sending the signal.

I think it's SIGINT vs SIGTERM. We default to SIGINT for stop and SIGTERM for kill.

That is not what docker does
```
man docker stop
...
SYNOPSIS
       docker stop [--help] [-t|--time[=10]] CONTAINER [CONTAINER...]

DESCRIPTION
       Stop a container (Send SIGTERM, and then SIGKILL after
        grace period)

```
Should be an easy fix then, assuming I can find where we set default stop signal
This is also overriden by the container image.

stopContainer does the following in oci.go
```
	stopSignal := ctr.config.StopSignal
	if stopSignal == 0 {
		stopSignal = uint(syscall.SIGTERM)
	}
```
The question is whether or not ctr.config.StopSignal is set correctly.

I'm testing with the Fedora image, which does not set stop signal itself.
We don't set a default for StopSignal anywhere in libpod, so it has to be in podman.
`podman inspect` confirms that StopSignal on containers is being set to 2 (SIGINT) if not explicitly set

In create.go, we should just remove 
	stopSignal := syscall.SIGINT
@rhatdan I just changed it to SIGTERM. PR coming soon.
Fixed by #299

A note for those, like me, new to podman who find this ticket.  Make sure that container PID 1 is not a shell (bash/dash/ash/sh) as this process will not pass SIGTERM to its child process(es).  A search for "ENTRYPOINT exec" should provide a good starting point on how to correct this.

Since `podman --log-level=debug stop mycontainer` does not print PIDs (as of `3.0.1`).  `strace` can be used to verify that SIGTERM is sent as specified.

```
# podman top mycontainer hpid,pid,comm
13261   1     sh
# strace -f podman kill -s TERM mycontainer
......
... pidfd_open(13261, 0)        = 3
......
... pidfd_send_signal(3, SIGTERM, NULL, 0) = 0
......
```
you can also specify a stop timeout of 0
For my application, SIGKILL could mean data loss.  So a clean shutdown is essential, however long it may take.
You can also go the other way and specify `-t 1000000`  or something. I am not sure if you can specify infinity.
how about having the application honor a signal?

That's definitely the core issue. If the application ignores the stop signal there isn't much Podman can do, and the rules around PID 1 in a namespace and signal handling do make things complicated in that regard (PID 1 must explicitly register a signal handler for the stop signal in order for this to work).
Is there a way to configure the default `-t 10` behaviour to `-t 0` globally at the system level?
Setting `stop_timeout=0` in `containers.conf` should accomplish that
